title.value: A SURVEY OF AUTOMATIC GENERATION OF SOURCE CODECOMMENTS: ALGORITHMS AND TECHNIQUES
title.evidence: A SURVEY OF AUTOMATIC GENERATION OF SOURCE CODECOMMENTS: ALGORITHMS AND TECHNIQUES

value: Xiaotao Song
Hailong Sun
Xu Wang
Jiafei Yan
authors.evidence:
Xiaotao Song School of Software Taiyuan University of Technology, China songxiaotao@tyut.edu.cn
Hailong Sun∗, Xu Wang and Jiafei Yan SKLSDE Lab, School of Computer Science and Engineering Beijing Advanced Innovation Center for Big Data and Brain Computing Beihang University, Beijing, China sunhl@buaa.edu.cn

abstract.value: As an integral part of source code files, code comments help improve program readability and comprehension. However, developers sometimes do not comment on their program code adequately due to the incurred extra efforts, lack of relevant knowledge, unawareness of the importance of code commenting or some other factors. As a result, code comments can be inadequate, absent or even mismatched with source code, which affects the understanding, reusing and the maintenance of software. To solve these problems of code comments, researchers have been concerned with generating code comments automatically. In this work, we aim at conducting a survey of automatic code commenting researches. First, we generally analyze the challenges and research framework of automatic generation of program comments. Second, we present the classification of representative algorithms, the design principles, strengths and weaknesses of each category of algorithms. Meanwhile, we also provide an overview of the quality assessment of the generated comments. Finally, we summarize some future directions for advancing the techniques of automatic generation of code comments and the quality assessment of comments.

To solve these problems of code comments, researchers have been concerned with generating code comments automatically. In this work, we aim at conducting a survey of automatic code commenting researches. First, we generally analyze the challenges and research framework of automatic generation of program comments. Second, we present the classification of representative algorithms, the design principles, strengths and weaknesses of each category of algorithms. Meanwhile, we also provide an overview of the quality assessment of the generated comments. Finally, we summarize some future directions for advancing the techniques of automatic generation of code comments and the quality assessment of comments.

value: Conduct a survey of automatic code commenting research.
Analyze the challenges and research framework of automatic generation of program comments.
Present the classification of representative algorithms and their strengths and weaknesses.
Provide an overview of the quality assessment of generated comments.
Summarize future directions for advancing techniques in automatic code comment generation.
research_objectives.evidence: In light of this, we aim at giving a comprehensive survey of the work on automatic code commenting for the following objectives: (1) giving researchers access to a catalogue of representative algorithms for automatic comment generation and providing new researchers with a good understanding of the state-of-the-art algorithms of automatic code commenting; (2) summarizing the main challenges and limitations of existing studies.

value: Building a source code model to express structural, lexical, grammatical, semantic, and context features of source code.
Generating satisfying code comments due to the inherent differences between programming languages and natural languages.
Quality assessment of generated comments, including unification of datasets and selection of evaluation criteria.
challenges.evidence: However, generating satisfying code comments remains a challenging issue. The fundamental reason lies in the fact that programming languages are different from natural languages in nature. The difference between code and comments is two-fold: source code contains a large amount of information about classes, methods and parameters of methods, and at the same time has many nested structures and complex invocation relations; meanwhile, comments written in natural languages are unstructured, and expressed freely in form. Consequently, automatic code commenting faces the following two challenges.

provenance.section_header: Challenges of Automatic Code Commenting and Research Framework

data_collection.value: Data collection involves constructing datasets for training, validating, and testing models by crawling or downloading from open source communities or websites, such as Stack Overflow.
data_collection.evidence: First, data collection to construct datasets for comment generation systems. These data are used for training, validating and testing models, extracting code and the corresponding comments, or extracting particular rules needed by a comment generation system.

provenance.section_header: Challenges of Automatic Code Commenting and Research Framework

value: Information Retrieval based algorithms
Deep Neural Networks based algorithms
Other automatic code comment generation algorithms
algorithm_classification.evidence: In this section first presents the classification of code commenting algorithms, then gives a thorough analysis of principles of each type of algorithms. Finally, we summarize the characteristics of the existing algorithms.

provenance.section_header: The algorithms of AUTOMATIC GENERATION of code comments

value: Manual assessment based on experienced developers evaluating generated comments.
Automatic assessment using metrics like BLEU, METEOR, ROUGE, and CIDEr.
evaluation_criteria.evidence: In summary, the comment quality assessment generally involves the following three perspectives: Experimental datasets, which refers to datasets that are used for evaluating code commenting algorithms. Evaluation methods, which generally include manual assessment and automatic assessment. Evaluation metrics, which refer to the criteria for evaluating the quality of comments.

provenance.section_header: QUALITY EVALUATION CRITERIA OF CODE COMMENTS

algorithm_performance.value: Deep neural network based comment generation algorithms have shown promising results but still face challenges in accuracy and comment sufficiency.
algorithm_performance.evidence: However, there still exist some drawbacks such as low accuracy of generated comments and insufficient generated comments on the whole.

quality_assessment.value: Quality assessment methods include manual evaluations by developers and automatic metrics like BLEU and METEOR, but there is a lack of unified datasets for comparison.
quality_assessment.evidence: However, there are no automatic quality assessment tool available exclusively for code comments, and researchers in software engineering field generally borrow natural language assessment criteria and tools.

provenance.section_header: QUALITY EVALUATION CRITERIA OF CODE COMMENTS

value: Exploring synergy between deep neural networks and other models.
Fusion of different source code models.
Designing a customized intelligent automatic comment generation system.
Unification of test datasets and comment quality assessment models.
future_directions.evidence: In the following discussion, we summarize the future research opportunities for automatic comment generation.
